flashcards.back: The degree to which a software product meets specified requirements and customer expectations. It encompasses functionality, reliability, usability, efficiency, maintainability, and portability.

flashcards.front: What are the six ISO 9126 quality characteristics?
flashcards.back: Functionality, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Maintainability, Portability - the international standard for software quality measurement.
Correctness, Reliability, Efficiency, Integrity, Usability, Maintainability, Testability, Flexibility, Portability, Reusability, Interoperability.

flashcards.front: What is the Cost of Quality (COQ)?
flashcards.back: Total cost incurred to prevent, detect, and correct defective work. Includes prevention costs, appraisal costs, and failure costs (internal and external).
The probability that software will perform its intended function without failure for a specified period under specified conditions. Measured in MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures).
The ease with which software can be modified to correct defects, improve performance, or adapt to changing requirements. Measured by mean time to repair (MTTR).
QA (Quality Assurance) is process-oriented - focuses on preventing defects. QC (Quality Control) is product-oriented - focuses on detecting and fixing defects.

flashcards.front: What is a Software Defect Life Cycle?
flashcards.back: States: New → Assigned → Open → Fixed → Verified → Closed. Can also include Rejected, Deferred, or Reopened states.
The extent to which software can be used by specified users to achieve specified goals with effectiveness, efficiency, and satisfaction in a specified context of use.
Verification: 'Are we building the product right?' (checking conformance to specifications). Validation: 'Are we building the right product?' (checking fitness for use).
Walkthrough, Technical Review, Inspection, Peer Review. These are static testing techniques to find defects early.
The degree to which test cases exercise the software code. Types: Statement coverage, Branch coverage, Path coverage, Condition coverage.
The ease with which software can be transferred from one environment to another. Includes hardware, software platform, or organizational environment changes.
Number of defects per thousand lines of code (KLOC) or per function point. Used to measure software quality - lower density indicates higher quality.
Capability Maturity Model Integration - a process improvement framework with 5 maturity levels: Initial, Repeatable, Defined, Quantitatively Managed, Optimizing.
